purpose: Links AI capex decisions to aggregate corporate profits and macro growth through accounting identity
components: Kalecki equation: Profits = Investment + Government Deficit + Net Exports - Household Saving
Federal deficit (~$1.8-2T) as dominant profit sustainer
AI capex (~$250B) as marginal growth contributor
25% capex cut → ~$60-75B profit reduction → ~2.5-3% S&P 500 earnings impact
Four-channel demand decomposition: capital income, complementary labor, consumer surplus, fiscal redistribution
current_assessment: The Kalecki identity transforms AI capex from a sector story to a macro story. The fiscal deficit is doing the heavy lifting, but AI capex is the marginal contributor responsible for profit growth.
limitations: Accounting identity does not specify causation or dynamics
Assumes investment term is independent of other terms (may not hold if capex cuts trigger fiscal response)
Single-channel underconsumption arguments are insufficient — the four-channel decomposition is required
Does not predict timing of capex cuts
